codeReview.summary: Feature implementation matches the requested scope: QUALITY_METHODOLOGY.md was added and README quality section was replaced with a concise summary + methodology link while keeping run commands. The methodology document covers all required sections (overview, categories, tiers, formula with worked example, evaluation approaches, tool-calling deep dive, contamination resistance, majority voting, CLI flags, extension guidance), and VAL-DOCS-001..005 expectations are satisfied.

sharedStateObservations.observation: python-worker skill procedure is overly strict for documentation-only features: it mandates TDD even when the task has no code logic. The worker had to explicitly deviate and note this in handoff feedback.
Shared architecture reference appears stale and no longer matches the current codebase structure, which can mislead workers during feature implementation/review.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:25-38,60-71,149 describes a problems/ package, CodeExecutionProblem, problem_source.py, and category-weight scoring, but current tree uses top-level *_problems.py files (mtb/quality_benchmarks directory listing), and scoring uses tier weights in mtb/quality_benchmarks/scoring.py:109 (TIER_WEIGHTS).

summary: PASS: documentation feature is complete and aligned with expected behavior/VAL-DOCS assertions; no blocking or non-blocking defects found in the implemented docs changes.
